steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Count the relevant faces, dimensions, or scale factor before setting up equations. Lengths scale by $k$, areas by $k^2$, and volumes by $k^3$; for boxes and prisms, write the needed face or volume formula directly.

steps.title: Set up the surface area formula
steps.content: A rectangular prism has three types of faces appearing twice each. With dimensions $l = 5$, $w = 6$, and $h = 7$ inches, we calculate the sum of the three distinct rectangular face areas, then double it.

steps.title: Calculate the three face areas
steps.content:
• Face 1 (5 x 6): $5 \times 6 = 30$ square inches
  • Face 2 (5 x 7): $5 \times 7 = 35$ square inches
  • Face 3 (6 x 7): $6 \times 7 = 42$ square inches
Sum: $30 + 35 + 42 = 107$ square inches.

steps.title: Multiply by 2 and identify the answer
steps.content:
The total surface area accounts for two of each face:
$$SA = 2 \times 107 = 214 \text{ square inches}$$That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
